GT LEARN #936 📝 Stamping The Sequence HARD Stack / Greedy
exp: [0,2]

📝 Problem Statement

You want to form the string target using a stamp. Initially, you have a string of '?' of length target.length. In each turn, you can place the stamp over the string and replace every letter with the corresponding stamped letter. Return the order of indices where you placed the stamp.

🧠 Solving Approach

Instead of building forward, we work backwards. We try to convert the target back into all '?'s. In each pass, we look for a window that matches the stamp (allowing '?' as wildcards). If found, we convert those chars to '?' and record the index.

Reverse Engineering Convert target → ??? instead of ??? → target.
Wildcard Match '?' in target matches any char in stamp.
Progress Check At least one non-'?' char must be converted per step.
Time: O(N(N-M)) Space: O(N)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

T1 Standard exp: [0,2]
stamp="abc" target="ababc"
T2 Impossible exp: []
stamp="ab" target="ba"
T3 Trivial exp: [0]
stamp="ab" target="ab"
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Reverse Stamping (start with ▶ to see first window check)
🎯 Target String (working copy — ? means already "unstamped" in reverse)
Letter (needs stamp) ? (stamped) Current Window Good (match or ?) Bad (mismatch)
🔍 Current Window Check / Replace
📚 Result Indices (collected during reverse pass — will be reversed at the end)
Output (Stamp Sequence)
0/0 INIT
←→ 100%
